name: API / backend microservice
summary: A headless, network-exposed service that fulfils a bounded capability behind a versioned API contract, called by other services or clients rather than by end users directly. Contract-first, stateless-where-possible, operated to explicit SLOs.

concerns: API contract definition (OpenAPI/JSON Schema/Protobuf/GraphQL SDL) as the source of truth
versioning strategy (URI/header/media-type) and deprecation/sunset policy with timelines
backward compatibility: additive-only evolution, no breaking changes to existing consumers, tolerant-reader expectations
consumer-driven contract testing and schema-compatibility checks in CI (breaking-change gate)
standardized error format (RFC 9457 problem+json), pagination, filtering, and content-negotiation conventions
questions: Is the API contract published and authoritative, with a versioning and deprecation/sunset policy?
Are backward-compatibility rules (additive-only, no breaking changes) enforced by contract/schema tests in CI?
Are standardized error responses (e.g. RFC 9457), pagination, and content negotiation specified?

concerns: authentication of callers: OAuth 2.0 / OIDC, JWT validation (signature, issuer, audience, expiry), mTLS for service-to-service
authorization: scopes/roles/claims enforced per endpoint; no broken object-level authorization (BOLA/IDOR) — OWASP API Security Top 10
input validation and output encoding at the boundary; injection and mass-assignment protection
secrets/token management, key rotation, and short-lived credentials
audit logging of security-relevant calls and abuse/anomaly detection
questions: Are caller authentication (OAuth/OIDC/JWT/mTLS) and per-endpoint, object-level authorization specified (no BOLA/IDOR)?
Is boundary input validation against the schema and injection/mass-assignment protection required?
Are token/secret rotation and audit logging of security-relevant calls addressed?

concerns: resilience patterns for downstream dependencies: timeouts, retries with exponential backoff + jitter, and circuit breakers
idempotency of mutating operations (idempotency keys) so safe retries do not double-apply effects
bulkheads and graceful degradation / fallback when a dependency is slow or down
availability SLO and error-budget policy; behaviour under partial outage of dependencies
questions: Are timeouts, bounded retries with backoff+jitter, and circuit breakers specified for every downstream dependency?
Are mutating operations idempotent (idempotency keys) so retries are safe?
Is an availability SLO with graceful degradation on dependency failure defined?

concerns: latency SLOs (p95/p99) and throughput (req/s) targets per endpoint
rate limiting / throttling and quota enforcement per client (429 + Retry-After), plus load-shedding under overload
connection/thread/pool sizing and resource ceilings; N+1 and payload-size efficiency
horizontal scalability and statelessness for autoscaling
questions: Are p95/p99 latency and throughput targets quantified per endpoint?
Are rate limiting, per-client quotas, and overload load-shedding specified?
Is the service horizontally scalable/stateless with defined resource ceilings?

concerns: observability: structured logs, RED/USE metrics, and distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) with trace/correlation propagation
automated test strategy: unit, integration, and consumer-driven contract tests as CI gates
modular bounded-context design and clear API/domain separation
documentation generated from the contract; changelog and SDK generation
questions: Are structured logging, metrics, and distributed tracing (with correlation IDs) required?
Are automated contract/integration tests enforced as CI gates?
Is API documentation generated from the contract and kept in sync?

concerns: health/readiness/liveness probes and graceful shutdown (connection draining) for zero-downtime deploys
configuration and secret injection via environment (12-factor), no hardcoded config
SLO monitoring, alerting on error budget burn, and runbooks; deploy/rollback and canary strategy
capacity/cost management and per-consumer usage metering
questions: Are health/readiness probes and graceful shutdown (draining) specified for zero-downtime deploys?
Is configuration/secret injection externalized (12-factor)?
Are SLO alerting, runbooks, and deploy/rollback/canary requirements defined?

concerns: request/response payload schemas, validation rules, and canonical data model ownership for the bounded context
database schema migration/versioning coordinated with API evolution (expand-contract)
data consistency model (strong vs eventual) and transaction boundaries across service calls
questions: Are request/response schemas and the service's owned data model defined?
Is schema migration coordinated with API versioning (expand-contract)?
Is the consistency model (strong/eventual) and transaction boundary specified?

concerns: completeness of the operations exposed for the bounded capability and their pre/post-conditions
correctness of state transitions and the exception/error contract per operation (not just the happy path)
concurrency semantics: optimistic concurrency (ETags/If-Match) and conflict handling
questions: Is every operation of the bounded capability specified with pre/post-conditions?
Is the error/exception behaviour (not only success) defined per operation?
Is concurrent-modification handling (optimistic concurrency/ETags) specified?
